key_risks: A hawkish FOMC message or upside inflation data could raise yields and trigger a sharp reversal in high-valuation growth and semiconductor equities.
Semiconductor and AI trades are crowded after very large trailing gains, increasing drawdown risk from profit-taking or positioning unwind.
South Korea and Taiwan exposures add currency, export-cycle, and regional geopolitical risks that could overwhelm sector momentum.
The reported U.S.-Iran peace progress could reverse or broader geopolitical tensions could flare, hurting global risk appetite.
A disappointing June retail sales, PCE, or CPI print could shift the market toward defensives and away from high-beta momentum.

portfolio.rationale: Strongest AI-linked leadership with exceptional 7-day, 30-day, 6-month, and 1-year momentum; should benefit most if risk appetite and growth leadership persist through the one-month window.
Extreme recent and longer-term momentum with meaningful semiconductor and export sensitivity, offering high beta to the same global tech cycle that has been leading markets.
Direct exposure to semiconductor supply-chain leadership and strong recent momentum, while diversifying geographically from US chip equities.
Broader AI technology exposure captures continued AI infrastructure and application enthusiasm with less single-industry concentration than semiconductors alone.
US momentum factor has strong 7-day and 30-day performance and can benefit from trend persistence in a low-volatility, risk-on tape.

portfolio_rationale: The portfolio concentrates in the strongest one-month leadership areas: AI, semiconductors, and semiconductor-linked Asian markets. With VIX subdued, equities rallying into the entry close, and geopolitical oil stress easing, the highest expected alpha over a one-month horizon appears to come from persistent momentum rather than defensive assets.
rationale_summary: Allocate aggressively to semiconductors, Korea, Taiwan, broad AI tech, and momentum to maximize upside if the current risk-on growth trend continues. The construction intentionally accepts high volatility to outperform the S&P 500 over the short scoring window.
